result.markdown:
---
title: "Replication: Beaudry and Portier (2014)"
subtitle: "News-Driven Business Cycles: Insights and Challenges"
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
bibliography: references.bib
---



## Overview

This document replicates the news shock identification of @beaudry2014 . A news shock is identified as the shock that has **no contemporaneous effect on TFP** but **maximizes the long-run response of TFP** at a 40-quarter horizon. The approach follows the mixed restriction strategy combining a zero impact restriction with a long-run maximization criterion.

## Setup


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
library(tictoc)
library(tidyMacro)

set_theme(ftheme_tidyMacro())
```
:::


## Data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
data("BeaudryPortier2014")

y        <- BeaudryPortier2014 |> as.matrix()
T_obs    <- nrow(y)
N        <- ncol(y)
varnames <- colnames(y)
```
:::


## VAR Estimation


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p <- 2
c <- 1

var_result <- fVAR(y, p = p, c = c)
Sigma <- var_result$sigma
```
:::


## News Shock Identification


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
horizon <- 40

# Wold IRFs by inverting (I - A(L))
wold <- fwoldIRF(var_result, horizon = horizon)

# Lower Cholesky factor of Sigma
S <- t(chol(Sigma))

# LR-Max point estimate
struct_irf <- fmaxIRF(wold, S, var_idx = 1L)
```
:::


## Bootstrap Confidence Bands


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
tic()
boot_max <- fbootstrapMaxCorrected(
  y          = y,
  var_result = var_result,
  nboot1     = 1000,
  nboot2     = 2000,
  horizon    = horizon,
  var_idx    = 1,          # maximise long-run effect on TFP (variable 1)
  cumulate   = integer(0), # no cumulation (levels data)
  prc        = 68          # Match original paper
)
toc()
#> 0.283 sec elapsed
```
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fplotirfLR(
  point       = struct_irf,
  boot_result = boot_max,
  varnames    = varnames,
  facet_ncol  = 2
) + labs(y  = NULL)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![IRF to Tech. news shock — LR-Max bootstrap (68% and 90% confidence bands)](BeaudryPortier2014_files/figure-html/fig-irf-boot-max-1.png){#fig-irf-boot-max width=100%}
:::
:::


## Compare the result to @barsky2012

Now we can compare the results to that of @barsky2012 and plot IRF results.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
struct_irf_uhlig <- fuhligIRF(wold, S, idx = 1L)

tic()
boot_uhlig <- fbootstrapUhligCorrected(
  y          = y,
  var_result = var_result,
  nboot1     = 1000,
  nboot2     = 2000,
  horizon    = horizon,
  idx        = 1L,        # maximise FEV share of TFP (variable 1)
  cumulate   = integer(0),
  prc        = 68         # Match original study
)
toc()
#> 0.271 sec elapsed
```
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fplotirfLR(
  point       = struct_irf_uhlig,
  boot_result = boot_uhlig,
  varnames    = varnames,
  facet_ncol  = 2
) + labs(y  = NULL)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![IRF to news shock — Uhlig max-share bootstrap (68% and 90% confidence bands)](BeaudryPortier2014_files/figure-html/fig-irf-boot-uhlig-1.png){#fig-irf-boot-uhlig width=100%}
:::
:::
